summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering hiring of multiple part-time firefighters/EMTs, approval of the 2023 property casualty insurance renewal at $105,528 (a 3% increase), and a detailed presentation of the draft 2023 budget. The board also accepted the resignation of Township Administrator Steve, appointed Bobby Rose as both Township Administrator and Police Chief, and approved a new Human Resource Manager job description following the resignation of the finance and HR director.
decisions: Motion to amend agenda to change item C dollar amount from $15.50 to $18 - passed
Motion to adopt agenda as amended - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills from September 28 through October 10, 2022 - passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/paramedic Ian Smith at $18/hour effective 10/23/22 - passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Elijah Man at $15.50/hour effective 10/23/22 - passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Shaler Newman at $15.50/hour effective 10/23/22 - passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Jerry Burns at $15.50/hour effective 10/23/22 - passed
Motion to accept $150 and food gift cards from Mounts Christian Church for police - passed
Motion to approve 2023 property casualty insurance renewal with Ohio Plan at $105,528 - passed
Motion to approve resignation of Township Administrator Steve - passed
Motion to approve job description for position of Police Chief and Township Administrator - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-53-2022 appointing Bobby Rose as Township Administrator and Police Chief - passed
Motion to approve job description for Human Resource Manager position and proceed with interviews - passed
Motion to adjourn at 9:15 PM - passed
action_items: Chad to have Christmas decorations up before Holly Fair on November 19th
Chad to contact Hannah about firm date for debris pile removal
Chad to trim tree line hanging over property near firehouse
Chad to assess property cleanup once debris pile is removed
Chad to email county about debris and text Hannah
Harry to continue monitoring Claremont subdivision development at county level
Harry to prepare zoning case for Barkley Wood Section 2 major adjustment for November 1 zoning commission and November 29 trustees meeting
Harry to advertise November 29 meeting at 6 PM with remote participation option
Steve/Cheryl to redo cemetery fee comparison with surrounding townships
Board to review draft 2023 budget over next two weeks for potential approval
Board to begin planning fire and ambulance levy renewal for 2023 ballot (levy expires 2024)
Post and interview candidates for Human Resource Manager position
Chad to purchase silver U-channel posts for street sign replacements
key_amounts: $18/hour - pay rate for part-time firefighter/paramedic Ian Smith
$15.50/hour - pay rate for part-time firefighter/EMTs (Elijah Man, Shaler Newman, Jerry Burns)
$150 plus food gift cards - donation from Mounts Christian Church to police
$100,000 - appropriated from unencumbered funds for tornado-related expenses
$237,000 - OPWC grant for paving (part of $500,000 project)
$1.4 million - fire and ambulance levy revenue (3.25 mil levy expiring 2024)
$250,000 - cost of ambulances purchased two years ago (now $325,000 for same model)
$90,000+ - projected zoning permit fee revenue for 2022 (budgeted $75,000)
$138,000 - projected unencumbered balance in EMS fund for next year
$1,105.63 - projected unencumbered balance in road and bridge fund
notable_topics: Hiring of four part-time firefighters/EMTs to fill part-time ranks
2023 property casualty insurance renewal with Ohio Plan
Detailed draft 2023 budget presentation across all township funds
Tornado recovery expenses and pending state disaster assistance reimbursement
Road and bridge fund financial concerns and declining revenue from gas tax and vehicle registrations
Fire and ambulance levy expiring in 2024 requiring renewal on 2023 ballot
Large residential subdivision development off State Route 48 (1-3 million dollar homes)
Barkley Wood Section 2 major PUD adjustment zoning case scheduled
Appointment of Bobby Rose as Township Administrator and Police Chief
Resignation of Finance and HR Director and creation of separate HR Manager position
Station 18 temporary operations and generator installation pending
Three officers completing probationary period (Item, McMillan/Detective, Baron)
Street sign U-channel post replacement (silver color selected)
Snowplow rodeo participation with OPWSO group
attendees: Bobby Rose (Police Chief, appointed Township Administrator)
notes: Significant leadership transition: Township Administrator Steve resigned to take a position at Hamilton Township, and the Finance/HR Director also resigned. Bobby Rose was appointed to dual role of Police Chief and Township Administrator. The meeting entered executive session from 7:56 PM to 9:10 PM to discuss employment matters. The tornado referenced throughout appears to have been a recent event significantly impacting township finances and operations. The Linton subdivision and a large development off SR 48 with homes ranging from $1-10 million represent significant potential growth for the township.
